reasoning: The Fed has maintained the target federal funds rate at 4.25-4.50% through its May 7, 2025 meeting, meaning there have been 0 rate cuts so far in 2025. For this market to resolve YES, there must be exactly 4 rate cuts (equivalent to 100 bps of standard 25-bps cuts) across the remaining ~5 meetings in the year. The CME FedWatch tool and consensus views from early to mid 2025 indicate markets expect only 1-2 rate cuts total by December, due to firm inflation prints and the Fed's cautious stance. A scenario with exactly 4 cuts would require a significant economic shift causing aggressive cutting, but without triggering so much panic that the Fed cuts 5+ times (which would resolve NO). The tight window of exactly 4 cuts is a narrow slice of the probability distribution. The Polymarket consensus was 6% in late May 2025, which accurately reflects this thin tail-risk path. With less time remaining and 0 cuts made so far, a projection of 4-5% is well-justified.
